definitions: Relating to an error in computer programming or mathematics where a loop or calculation iterates one time too many or too few, or accesses an array element one position too high or too low.
Characterized by a discrepancy of exactly one unit or position.
examples: The programmer fixed the off-by-one error in the loop.
It's a common off-by-one mistake when dealing with array indices.
The off-by-one calculation led to an incorrect total.
